findings.message: Added `requests` with no version constraint. All other runtime dependencies carry a `>=X.Y` floor; `requests` has no floor at all, so any version (including ancient, vulnerable releases) can be resolved. Pin a minimum version (e.g., `>=2.32`) to match the project's pinning convention and avoid resolving EOL releases.

`httpx>=0.28` is already in the dev dependency group and is the idiomatic async HTTP client for FastAPI services. For runtime HTTP calls in an async context, `httpx` (async-capable) is preferred over `requests` (synchronous-only), which can block the event loop. If `requests` is only needed in tests or scripts, move it to the `dev` group; if needed at runtime, consider replacing with `httpx` promoted to the main dependencies.
findings.suggestion: Promote `httpx` to the `[project] dependencies` list and remove `requests`, or justify why the synchronous client is required at runtime.
